name: Niacin (Vitamin B3)

sources.title: Niacin Fact Sheet for Health Professionals (NIH ODS)
FDA niacin safety communications (formulation-dependent risk context)

description: Nicotinic acid is a nutrient precursor for NAD/NADP and also used pharmacologically for dyslipidemia at high doses.
summary: Essential vitamin B3 nutrient with drug-like high-dose lipid effects; formulation and dose strongly affect safety.
mechanism: Nutritional role: NAD/NADP precursor. Pharmacologic role: GPR109A-related lipolysis/lipid effects.
safetyNotes: 1–3 g/day can cause flushing, GI effects, and hepatotoxicity.
interactions: Can increase myopathy risk with statins at pharmacologic doses.
dosage: Adult RDA ~14–16 mg/day; pharmacologic dyslipidemia regimens ~1–3 g/day.
preparation: Immediate-, extended-, and sustained-release products differ in risk profile.
regulatoryStatus: Essential nutrient; also FDA-approved in prescription lipid indications.
sourceType: Vitamin nutrient and pharmacologic lipid agent
